_meta.variant_delta_from_v8: Two factors bundled: (a) validator general_min_post_count=100 for CAT_GENERAL exact matches, (b) reject_meta_non_whitelist=True for CAT_META tags. Otherwise identical to V8.

verdict: V10 REJECTED. Floor=100 + meta filter marginally regresses on every prompt. The niche-tag pollution the user flagged was at 500-3000 post counts, not under 100 — our floor didn't catch the real culprits. Meta filter barely activated because most odd tags were CAT_GENERAL variants, not CAT_META.
lessons: Popularity floor needs to be calibrated against the actual post_count of observed bad tags, not guessed. User's flagged tags (sparse_leg_hair=716, heavy=787, partially_shaded_face=3350) would survive floor=100 and even floor=500. A meaningful floor would be 1000+, but that risks dropping legitimate descriptive tags.
Two-factor bundle made attribution impossible. Should have tested meta filter alone vs popularity floor alone. If we pursue this axis again, separate.

The 'niche-tag pollution' axis may not be the highest-impact one remaining — V8 was +0.2 over baseline with no regression, V10 is -0.175 from V8. Time to assess whether remaining issues are worth variant iteration or whether V8 is the ship candidate.
